behavior_summary: Provides an examination ('U8P_BROCK::look') handler for the BROCK object that reports a short identifying string based on the item's current frame/state (maps frames 0..4 to specific artifact piece names).

Contains a complex helper activation routine that appears to implement multi-stage artifact interactions or a dramatic scripted event: scanning references, applying damage to nearby targets, spawning visual/sound effects, creating/destroying temporary items, changing the BROCK item's frame/type, and performing camera/fade/teleport operations.
The U8P_BROCK::look handler is read-only and presentation-focused (text bark). The helper performs gameplay-affecting changes and large-scale scene effects (damage, teleport, stasis, camera quake).

Most external engine routines (Item::*, spawn, playSFX, process LightningBolt, Camera::startQuake, FadeToBlack/FadeFromBlack, Npc::teleport, etc.) are used by the helper to stage visual and mechanical outcomes.
notable_control_flow: BROCK::look branches on Item::getFrame(item_ptr) (frame 0..4) to select the bark string, then suspends — straightforward event-to-text mapping.

U8P_BROCK::BROCK::0197 (helper) branches heavily on the item's type/frame and on the triggering process state, iterates item reference slots (Item::push / loopscr / Item::getQ), and uses loop counters to apply repeated effects (Item::receiveHit with urandom-based damage) to enumerated targets.

The helper interleaves asynchronous visual processes (spawn PYROS/LITHOS/FREE), synchronous operations (Item::create/Item::destroy, Item::setFrame), and global scene transitions (FadeToBlack/FadeFromBlack, Camera quakes, setAvatarInStasis) to construct a multi-step scripted sequence.
Reference resets (Kernel::resetRef) and pop/popToEnd calls are used to manage and clean up referenced helper items created during sequences.
open_questions: What exact game-state conditions or item type constants trigger each distinct branch inside U8P_BROCK::BROCK::0197 (the disassembly shows many branches but not the high-level design intent for artifact progression vs. defeat sequence)?
Which external spawn processes (FREE::1C19, PYROS::1914, LITHOS::0CCD, etc.) correspond to specific visual effects, and how do their parameters alter the appearance/behavior of the sequence?
Does BROCK::0197 get invoked by a player 'use' action, another event type, or an external script? The entry event for the helper routine isn't explicit in the disassembly summary.

Are there global quest flags or persistent state changes outside the script's local item frame/type changes that should be set during the activation sequence (no global flags were observed being written in the provided extract)?

main_function.name: U8P_BROCK::look
main_function.purpose: Handle 'U8P_BROCK::look' (examine) events for the BROCK object and emit a short identifying line describing which artifact piece the item currently represents, based on its frame value.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the object's 'U8P_BROCK::look' / examine event (Event 0 or equivalent engine 'U8P_BROCK::look' trigger).
Requires the viewer/trigger process id (pid) and the item instance reference (item_ptr).

function_map.name: U8P_BROCK::look
function_map.role: Examine handler that reads the item's frame (Item::getFrame) and uses Item::bark to display a descriptive string (frames 0..4 map to specific artifact piece names).

function_map.name: U8P_BROCK::0197
function_map.role: Complex activation/interaction routine: inspects equipment/target, branches on item type/frame, enumerates and manipulates referenced items, applies hits/damage, spawns visual processes, creates/destroys items, sets frames/types, and orchestrates camera/fade/teleport effects for a scripted event.

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Engine call returning an item's current frame/state used for branching and presentation.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit a short text line (dialogue/description) on behalf of an item/process to a player/process.

external_calls.purpose: Read the BROCK item's frame/state to decide which description to display.
Display a short identifying string to the viewer (pid) describing the artifact piece.
Inspect an NPC equip slot to guard or conditionally alter behavior based on the actor's equipment/state.
Query the item's type id to route to different scripted outcomes.
Enumerate and manage the item's referenced slots/contained items in order to find targets and to create/clean up helper objects.
Create temporary helper items at the BROCK's coordinates, update their state or the BROCK's frame/type, and destroy temporary objects during cleanup.
Start asynchronous visual/effect processes to accompany scripted events (explosions, flames, rock effects).
Apply damage/hits to targeted referenced entities with randomized damage values and trigger lightning/damage effects.
Play sound effects at key moments of the scripted sequence for dramatic feedback.
Query the BROCK item's coordinates to position created helper items and effects appropriately.

external_calls.callee: process FadeToBlack / FadeFromBlack / Camera::startQuake / Camera::stopQuake / setAvatarInStasis / Npc::teleport
external_calls.purpose: Perform scene-level transitions (fade, camera quake, stasis) and teleportation to deliver a cinematic scripted outcome.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides an examination ('U8P_BROCK::look') handler for the BROCK object that reports a short identifying string based on the item's current frame/state (maps frames 0..4 to specific artifact piece names).
